description:
Advances: mutagen.scope_resolution, mutagen.test_selection, mutagen.mutation_enumeration, mutagen.mutation_pipeline, mutagen.coverage

**MEGA-TICKET** — bundle of interdependent AST/perf work. Splits cleanly only at the cost of duplicating shared helper work across several smaller tickets. Themed bundle per the landing-plan spec.

Closes these review findings:

- **F15** (HIGH, Perf F-PERF-04): Whole-file `Code.compile_quoted` × 2 per site. Pre-cache original `.beam` so restore is `:code.load_binary/3`.
- **F16** (HIGH, Perf F-PERF-02): Whole-file `Macro.prewalk` per site. Batched grouped-by-file prewalk; one walk per file regardless of sites.
- **F18 / CF7** (HIGH, Sec + Perf): Baseline.detect_async_modules re-reads test files outside AST cache.
- **F19 / CF7** (HIGH, Perf F-PERF-06): TestSelector.scan_for_tag walks the entire test tree on every invocation.
- **F20** (HIGH, Perf): phase_scope O(n²) `acc ++ records` (half-overlap with .22 — if .22 lands first, this becomes a no-op for that finding).
- **F21 / CF10** (HIGH, Idioms M1 + Perf F-PERF-08): Triplicated AST helpers across ScopeResolver / MutationEnumerator / MutationRunner. Extract `MutagenEx.Ast` (or `AstHelpers`).
- **F40 / CF7** (MED, Perf F-PERF-15): AST cache scope-only. Extend to cited test files.
- **F45 / CF7** (MED, Sec M2 + Perf): Default `--scope` resolution re-walks `lib/**/*.ex` per target.

## Deliverable

- New `lib/mutagen_ex/ast.ex` with canonical `alias_to_module/1`, `find_module_body/2`, `walk_body/N`, `node_line/1` etc. Delete duplicates from scope_resolver.ex, mutation_enumerator.ex, mutation_runner.ex.
- Extend `lib/mutagen_ex/ast_cache.ex` to cover scope files AND cited test files in a single load step. Update Mix tasks `phase_load_ast/N` to populate the unified cache.
- Replace per-site `Macro.prewalk` in mutation_runner.ex:372-387 with grouped-by-file batched prewalk + head-atom dispatch table for mutator selection (`%{ :+ => [Arith], :case => [CaseDrop], ...}`).
- Pre-cache original `.beam` artifacts at compile time so `restore/3` becomes `:code.load_binary/3` not `Code.compile_quoted` (cuts the restore path from a recompile to a binary swap).
- Fix `phase_scope` accumulation: prepend + reverse.
- Wrap `Path.wildcard("lib/**/*.ex")` results in `Enum.sort/1` (F30 / CF7 determinism contract — small but lands cleanly here).
- Revise affected specs: scope_resolution, test_selection, mutation_enumeration, mutation_pipeline, coverage. Document the unified AST cache contract.

## Verification

- All existing tests green
- Bench harness in priv/helper_scripts/ measuring per-site time before / after (target: 2-4× speedup on representative scope)
- Memory test: large scope (1000 sites) does not blow heap
- mix compile --warnings-as-errors green

## Allowed touches

- lib/mutagen_ex/ast.ex (new)
- lib/mutagen_ex/ast_cache.ex
- lib/mutagen_ex/scope_resolver.ex
- lib/mutagen_ex/test_selector.ex
- lib/mutagen_ex/baseline.ex
- lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_enumerator.ex
- lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner.ex (only the `Macro.prewalk` site + helpers; not the lifecycle / restore wrapping from .17)
- lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex (phase_scope fix + ast cache wiring)
- All affected spec files
- Corresponding test files
- priv/helper_scripts/ (bench harness)
- README.md, CHANGELOG.md

## Out of Scope

- Do not change MutationLoop or Application supervision (those are .18)
- Do not change mutator behaviour API (touches mutators/*.ex only if a head-atom dispatch table needs to expose it)
- Do not introduce parallelism (.30 territory)
- Do not stream the report (.30)

## Recommended planning path

This is the largest single ticket in the landing plan. Recommend running /mega-plan against this ticket before /implement. The kernel is in 00-spec.md.

## Merge gate
- [ ] mix compile --warnings-as-errors green
- [ ] mix test green
- [ ] Determinism: two consecutive runs produce byte-identical JSON
- [ ] Bench harness shows measurable speedup (document the number)
- [ ] No new public API on `MutagenEx.Ast` beyond the documented helpers
- [ ] Auditor APPROVE

comments.text:
ORCHESTRATOR_ESCALATION: skipping this ticket and its dependent mutagen-wrd.29.

Reason: the ticket spec explicitly says "Recommended planning path: This is the largest single ticket in the landing plan. Recommend running /mega-plan against this ticket before /implement." Orchestrator policy forbids running /mega-plan autonomously — needs user driver.

Orchestrator action:
- mutagen-wrd.25 left open, no impl worker spawned
- mutagen-wrd.29 left open (blocked_by .25)
- All other open children processed via normal pipeline

When user is ready, run /mega-plan against mutagen-wrd.25 to produce a phased subplan, then re-orchestrate (or /implement the resulting decomposed tickets).

Orchestrator carved 2 scope-expansion subtickets surfaced during .25.3 execution: .25.8 (production phase_baseline cache wiring — capability landed in .25.3, runtime wiring deferred per .25.3's Allowed-touches scope) and .25.9 (flaky atom-count test originated in .25.2, surfaced during .25.3 rebase). Neither blocks the .25.5–.25.7 critical path.

DELIVER: closing epic.

All 7 critical-path children (.25.1–.25.7) closed via orchestrator verify/audit/merge-back pipeline. Epic branch epic/mutagen-wrd preserved at 0db66a0 awaiting human-checkpoint PR review against main.

Open follow-ups (intentional, non-blocking; kept open):
- mutagen-wrd.25.8 (S2-fu: wire ast_cache into phase_baseline production path) — capability landed in .25.3; runtime wiring scope-expanded per orchestrator authority since .25.3 Allowed-touches excluded the wiring file
- mutagen-wrd.25.9 (BUG: flaky atom-count test r8 invariant order-dependence) — pre-existing flake originating in .25.2, surfaced during .25.3 rebase
- mutagen-wrd.36 (Triage .25 speedup shortfall to 2-4x target) — filed at mutagen-wrd level per .25.7 spec "if speedup < 2x" clause; observed 1.66x vs 2-4x target

Closing .25 unblocks mutagen-wrd.29 and mutagen-wrd.31.

ORCHESTRATED
Children closed: 7 (.25.1, .25.2, .25.3, .25.4, .25.5, .25.6, .25.7)
BOUNCEs encountered: 2
  - mutagen-wrd.25.2 iter 2: missing falsifiability for scope_resolution.r9 + retroactive authorization for test_module_discovery.ex
  - mutagen-wrd.25.5 iter 2: structural-only coverage on r16 site.id-keying + s14 prewalk-count clauses (needed dedicated tests)
User escalations: 0
Audit-expanded scope authorizations: 2 retroactive (.25.2 + .25.6)
Scope-expansion follow-up subtickets filed: 2 + 1
  - mutagen-wrd.25.8 (S2-fu prod wiring; non-blocking)
  - mutagen-wrd.25.9 (flaky atom-count test root-cause; non-blocking)
  - mutagen-wrd.36 (speedup-shortfall triage; filed by .25.7 worker per ticket spec)
Bookkeeping commits on epic branch: 2
  - 792acea (mega-plan spec + 5 decisions)
  - b343109 (mix format fix on ast_donor_equivalence_test.exs)
Final epic-branch tip: 0db66a0
Critical-path test count: 517 → 628 (+111 across the 7 stages)

Epic branch is ready for a single PR to main — deliberate human checkpoint per workflow rules. Orchestrator did NOT push or open a PR.

title: MEGA: AST/perf consolidation — MutagenEx.Ast + extended cache + batched prewalk + .beam restore
